channel.title: CyberWatch Threat Intelligence
channel.description: Aggregated CVEs, advisories, incidents and IOCs

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: k9s-0.51.0-2.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-4890ba29de)
item.description: Bump golang.org/x/crypto to v0.52.0 and golang.org/x/net to v0.55.0

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: k9s-0.51.0-2.fc43 (FEDORA-2026-d6f3a0fa5a)
item.description: Bump golang.org/x/crypto to v0.52.0 and golang.org/x/net to v0.55.0

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: ansible-collection-ansible-posix-2.2.1-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-4a0c61a43f)
item.description: Automatic update for ansible-collection-ansible-posix-2.2.1-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Wed Jul 8 2026 Maxwell G - 2.2.1-1 - Update to 2.2.1. Fixes rhbz#2479556. - Mitigates CVE-2026-11837 (rhbz#2487430, rhbz#2487431, rhbz#2487432) ```

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: xorg-x11-server-Xwayland-24.1.13-1.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-a3d32667b8)
item.description: Update to xwayland 24.1.13, fixes for: CVE-2026-55999, CVE-2026-56000

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: python-safetensors-0.8.0-2.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-9db41d0ada)
item.description: Update safetensors to [0.8.0](https://github.com/safetensors/safetensors/releases#release-v0.8.0) and build the Python extension using PyO3 0.29, which fixes RUSTSEC-2026-0176 and RUSTSEC-2026-0177.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: pam-1.7.2-2.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-377015b34b)
item.description: pam_userdb: fix password comparison timing leak

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: python-uv-build-0.11.28-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-118fdf066a)
item.description: https://github.com/astral-sh/uv/releases/tag/0.11.28

item.title: [HIGH] Fedora: attr-2.6.0-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-c7aafa6056)
item.description: Automatic update for attr-2.6.0-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Thu Jul 9 2026 Lukáš Zaoral - 2.6.0-1 - rebase to the latest upstream release (rhbz#2494157) - CVE-2026-54371 - Symlink Traversal Privilege Escalation via getfattr (rhbz#2494172) - remove a long-overdue workaround for /usr/include/attr/xattr.h ```

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-43499
item.description: OPPO Find N2 GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499) exploit adaptation

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-29519
item.description: Proof of Concept for Reflected XSS in Lucee CFML (CVE-2026-29519)

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2026-41089
item.description: CVE-2026-41089 checker: unauthenticated, non-destructive detection for the Netlogon CLDAP stack buffer overflow (CVSS 9.8). Reports whether a domain controller's domain is long enough to crash, without sending the overflow. The binary-verified analysis the public PoCs got wrong.

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2025-22457
item.description: Altay takımı haftalık sunumu için yaptığım cve-2025-22457 zafiyeti demo uygulaması

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2025-8110
item.description: Ghostlink HTB — Full Chain AD + Gogs Exploitation | MQTT → NTLM Relay → Path Traversal → CVE-2025-8110 → ADCS ESC11 → DCSync | AMN SECURITY

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-56423
item.description: PoC for CVE-2026-56423: MISP deleteSelection broken access control (CWE-862, contributor hard-deletes other orgs' Event Reports/Sharing Groups, CVSS 8.8)

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-46331
item.description: A Proof of Concept (PoC) exploit for CVE-2026-46331

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2025-55182
item.description: ☢️ ReactorWatch v3.2.1 — Nuclear Reactor Core Monitoring System Pentest | CVE-2025-55182 (React2Shell) Unauthenticated RCE → SQLite Exfil → MD5 Crack → SSH Pivot → CDP Root Escalation | CVSS 10.0 | AMN SECURITY

item.title: [MEDIUM] Arch Linux: Arch Issue
item.description: Type: | Severity: Unknown | Package:

item.title: [MEDIUM] VMware: VMSA-2026-0004: VMware Cloud Foundation Operations updates address multiple vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-41722, CVE-2026-41723 and CVE-2026-41724)
[LOW] CVE-2026-53433: fzf is vulnerable to a Denial of Service (DoS) due to inefficient HTTP body proc...
item.description: fzf is vulnerable to a Denial of Service (DoS) due to inefficient HTTP body processing in the --listen mode due to inefficient HTTP body processing using repeated string concatenation, resulting in quadratic time complexity (O(n²)). A crafted POST request with many small segments can trigger excessive CPU usage during request handling.This allows a single malicious request to monopolize the single

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-53432: fzf is vulnerable to Integer Overflow leading to crash in FuzzyMatchV2 function....
item.description:
fzf is vulnerable to Integer Overflow leading to crash in FuzzyMatchV2 function. When input line length is approximately 2,200,000 bytes and pattern length is 999 bytes, the product overflows. The Go runtime detects the invalid slice bounds and terminates the process immediately with a non-recoverable panic.
This issue was fixed in version 0.73.1.

item.title: [HIGH] CVE-2026-4629: A flaw was found in Keycloak. A highly privileged user with `manage-clients` per...
item.description: A flaw was found in Keycloak. A highly privileged user with `manage-clients` permission can exploit this vulnerability by injecting a hardcoded role mapper into any client. This action allows the user to bypass existing scope restrictions and inject the `realm-admin` role into generated tokens, resulting in privilege escalation and full administrative access to the realm.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-47105: Rejected reason: This CVE ID has been rejected or withdrawn by its CVE Numbering...
item.description: Rejected reason: This CVE ID has been rejected or withdrawn by its CVE Numbering Authority.

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-44946: A SAML authentication replay vulnerability in Rancher's Assertion Consumer Serv...
item.description: A SAML authentication replay vulnerability in Rancher's Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) handler did not enforce one-time use of SAML assertion, potentially allowing person in the middle attacks against Rancher, affecting Rancher 2.14.0 before 2.14.3,

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-14209: A vulnerability was discovered in Keycloak's Admin UI extension that allows cert...
item.description: A vulnerability was discovered in Keycloak's Admin UI extension that allows certain administrative users to bypass security restrictions. When Fine-Grained Admin Permissions (FGAPv2) are enabled, an administrator who should only be able to search for users (but not view their full details) can use a specific "brute-force-user" endpoint to access a user's full profile. This includes sensitive infor

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-13474: Denial of service via malformed HTTP/2 requests in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler G...
item.description: Denial of service via malformed HTTP/2 requests in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway if HTTP/2 is enabled in HTTP Profile and associated with the virtual server (of type LB, CS, VPN) or the service configured on NetScaler

item.title: [HIGH] CVE-2026-12388: A flaw was found in the Identity Provider (IdP) mapper component of Keycloak, wh...
item.description: A flaw was found in the Identity Provider (IdP) mapper component of Keycloak, which is used to manage how user information from external services is mapped to Keycloak users. An administrator with limited permissions to manage identity providers can exploit this flaw by creating a "Hardcoded Role" mapper that assigns high-level administrative roles (like realm-admin) to themselves or others. This

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-10817: Insufficient input validation leading to memory overread in NetScaler ADC and Ne...
item.description: Insufficient input validation leading to memory overread in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway if the TCP TimeStamp is enabled in TCP Profile and is associated with the virtual server (of type LB, CS, VPN) or the service configured on NetScaler

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-10816: Arbitrary File Read (Unauthenticated) in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway if ...
item.description: Arbitrary File Read (Unauthenticated) in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway if the access to NSIP, Cluster Management IP or SNIP with management access is enabled

item.title: [MEDIUM] AI Coding: Do Security Risks Outweigh Productivity Gains?
item.description: AI coding tools cost $19-$200/month/user, but security scanning, remediation, and false positives add hidden costs. Are the productivity gains worth it?

item.title: [HIGH] Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang
item.description: Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, was sentenced to 70 months for helping the BlackCat/Alphv group. The post Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [CRITICAL] Hackers Can Go From CitrixBleed 2 Exploitation to Ransomware in Under an Hour
item.description: A critical Citrix flaw is giving intruders a fast route from an internet-facing gateway to a ransomware event. The activity centers on CitrixBleed 2, tracked as CVE-2025-5777, which can expose memory from affected NetScaler ADC and Gateway appliances before a user signs in. That exposure lets attackers search for and reuse active session tokens. The […] The post Hackers Can Go From CitrixBleed 2 Exploitation to Ransomware in Under an Hour appeared first on Cyber Security News.

item.title: [CRITICAL] Malicious Windows Shortcuts Use PowerShell and Node.js to Enable Remote Code Execution
item.description: A malicious Windows shortcut is being used to turn a routine download into a full remote-code-execution foothold. The campaign begins with convincing booking-themed spam and steers victims toward a ZIP archive that conceals a booby-trapped LNK file. One click can quietly start a chain that installs a backdoor and gives attackers a path to run […] The post Malicious Windows Shortcuts Use PowerShell and Node.js to Enable Remote Code Execution appeared first on Cyber Security News.

item.title: [HIGH] GNU Guix Vulnerabilities Allow Remote Privilege Escalation via Malicious Binary Substitutes
item.description: GNU Guix has disclosed four serious security vulnerabilities affecting its package substitution and channel-management features. Three flaws in the guix substitute utility can enable remote privilege escalation, corruption of stored data, and local disclosure of files readable by the build daemon user. At the same time, a fourth issue affects guix pull and guix time-machine. […] The post GNU Guix Vulnerabilities Allow Remote Privilege Escalation via Malicious Binary Substitutes appeared fi

item.title: [LOW] Linux Kernel FUSE Vulnerability Lets Attackers Gain Root Privileges
item.description: A Linux kernel vulnerability in the FUSE subsystem can allow a local attacker to gain root privileges by overflowing the page cache with attacker-controlled directory entries. The flaw is tracked as CVE-2026-31694 and affects the code path used when the kernel caches FUSE readdir results. FUSE lets a userspace filesystem talk to the kernel through […] The post Linux Kernel FUSE Vulnerability Lets Attackers Gain Root Privileges appeared first on Cyber Security News.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Hackers are Turning AI Gateways as Attack Surfaces to Compromise Enterprise Networks
item.description: AI gateways are increasingly being targeted as organizations connect generative AI applications to cloud services such as Amazon Bedrock. These gateways sit between users, business applications, and large language models, making them an attractive entry point into enterprise networks. Darktrace recently investigated a compromised Amazon Web Services EC2 instance named “LiteLLM-Proxy.” The instance appeared to […] The post Hackers are Turning AI Gateways as Attack Surfaces to Compromise Ent

item.title: [CRITICAL] China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force
item.description: Both foes and allies have targeted the Balochistan Police force in Pakistan for at least two years, according to SentinelOne. The post China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [HIGH] Can AI-generated adversaries break TTP-based attribution? (arXiv 2026)
item.description: Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) has traditionally attributed attacks through Tactics, Techniques and Procedures (TTPs). In this paper we evaluate whether that assumption still holds when AI agents are explicitly configured to emulate known threat groups. We configured AI agents to reproduce the behavior of APT28, APT29, APT41, APT44 and Lazarus inside enterprise and military cyber ranges. Our resu

item.title: [MEDIUM] Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
item.description: A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch. FoxIO researcher Sébastien Féry disclosed the flaw on July 8 and nicknamed it XRING. He says it needs no login and no malformed packets: about 260 bytes of ordinary QPACK traffic takes the server

item.title: [CRITICAL] Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
item.description: The Zimbra security team urged customers to patch a critical vulnerability affecting the Classic Web Client used to access the Zimbra Collaboration suite. [...]
